reasons: Shipped production hybrid retrieval + ranking to ~2M MAU at a product company — hits the JD's central must-have.
Designed rigorous offline (NDCG@10, MRR) and online A/B evaluation with a measured 14% engagement lift.
Strong, multi-source-corroborated Python and FastAPI signal (Redrob 91, LeetCode 540, endorsements).
Genuinely available: recently active, 78% recruiter response rate, 30-day notice, willing to relocate to Pune.
reservations: Self-listed LLM fine-tuning (LoRA) is contradicted by absence of corroborating projects — do not credit fine-tuning depth.
No managed vector-database (Pinecone/Weaviate/Qdrant) operational experience; relies on FAISS + OpenSearch.
The 14% A/B lift is self-reported and not independently verified.

interview_focus.rationale: Verify operational depth on embedding drift, index refresh, and retrieval-quality regression — explicit JD must-have.
suggested_questions: Walk me through how you detected and handled embedding drift in the hybrid-ranker system.
How did you refresh the FAISS/OpenSearch index without serving stale results?

interview_focus.rationale: Confirm offline-to-online correlation reasoning behind the reported 14% lift.
suggested_questions: How well did your offline NDCG/MRR predict the online A/B outcome?
What guardrail metrics did you watch during the A/B test?

interview_focus.rationale: Addresses the contradicted self-listed LoRA claim and the nice-to-have gap.
suggested_questions: Describe any hands-on fine-tuning work; when would you fine-tune vs. prompt for reranking?

missing_evidence: Independent verification of the online A/B 14% lift.

recommendations.rationale: Rare, well-corroborated production search/ranking fit who is genuinely available; move quickly.
Technical screen on retrieval operations and evaluation rigor to confirm self-reported metrics.

summary: Strong hire. Production-proven search/ranking backend engineer matching the core must-haves with strong Python and real availability; only minor nice-to-have gaps and one recorded, down-weighted contradiction (unsubstantiated LoRA claim).
